title: aeschynomene mossoensis var. parvifolia
intro: Aeschynomene mossoensis var. parvifolia, also known as the parvifolia or small-leaved horsefly-pea, is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae. It is a shrub-like plant with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems. The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Aeschynomene mossoensis var. parvifolia can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, typically using the plant's leaves and stems, to make herbal teas or infusions that can be consumed for its medicinal properties.
